title: THE METHODOLOGICAL ASPECTS OF USING REAL-TIME POLYMERASE CHAIN REACTION (RT-PCR) IN BRETTANOMYCES/DEKKERA DETECTION
author: Mitina, Irina
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Wine spoilage could be caused by a lot of genera and species of yeasts. One of the most damaging is  Brettanomyces/Dekkera  which causes severe quality problems in the wine industry. Timely detection of these organisms in wine is of crucial importance for preserving wine quality. The conventional methods of diagnostics are often time-consuming and laborious. For this, molecular-based methods of rapid testing, particularly real-time PCR are gaining increasing importance. In this work, the potential of the real-time PCR method in microbiological monitoring of wines was evaluated, and analysis process according to the PIKA Weihenstephan TM  SO Detection Kit H  Brettanomyces/Dekkera  protocol was optimized.

In this work, the potential of the real-time PCR method in microbiological monitoring of wines was evaluated, and analysis process according to the PIKA Weihenstephan TM  SO Detection Kit H  Brettanomyces/Dekkera  protocol was optimized.
